term.abilityScores: The six scores that define your hero, from 3 (feeble) to 20 (legendary). What matters most is the modifier in front: you add it to nearly every roll you make.
term.armorClass: How hard you are to hit. An attacker rolls a d20 and adds its bonuses; it hits only if the total meets or beats this number. Armor, shields, and Dexterity raise it.
term.attackRoll: Roll a d20 and add this bonus; you hit if the total meets or beats the target's Armor Class. The bonus is your ability modifier plus your Proficiency Bonus; damage adds only the ability modifier. On a Critical Hit you roll all the attack's damage dice twice (including extra dice like Sneak Attack) and total them; your modifier is still added only once.
term.initiative: When combat starts, everyone rolls a d20 and adds this bonus. The results set the turn order: highest acts first.
term.speed: How far you can move on your turn, alongside your action. Difficult terrain costs double.
term.proficiencyBonus: Your training bonus. Add it to every roll you are proficient in: trained skills, saving throws, weapon attacks, and your spells. It grows with level, from +2 at level 1 to +6 at level 20.
term.hitPoints: Your stamina and will to keep fighting. Damage lowers them; rest and healing restore them. At 0 you fall unconscious and start making death saving throws.
term.hitDice: Your personal healing pool, one die per level. During a Short Rest you can spend Hit Dice: roll each die, add your Constitution modifier, and regain that many hit points.
term.passiveScore: What you notice without rolling: 10 plus the skill's bonus. The DM checks it quietly, for example to see whether you spot a hidden creature.
term.savingThrow: A d20 roll to resist a danger: dodging a fireball, shaking off a charm, enduring poison. The threat decides which ability you use; proficiency adds your Proficiency Bonus.
term.skillCheck: When the outcome is uncertain, the DM calls for a check: roll a d20 and add the skill's bonus. The dot marks skills you are proficient in.
term.spellSaveDc: How hard your spells are to resist. When a spell forces a saving throw, the target rolls a d20 plus its bonuses and must meet or beat this number.
term.spellAttack: The bonus you add to a d20 when a spell asks for an attack roll against a target's Armor Class, like a hurled flame.
term.spellSlot: The fuel for casting. Each spell of level 1 or higher spends one slot of that level or higher; cantrips are free. Slots return when you rest.
term.cantrip: A minor spell you can cast at will, without spending a spell slot. It never needs preparing; it is always ready.
term.preparedSpells: The spells you currently have ready to cast. After a Long Rest you can swap which spells you prepare, choosing from your class list.
term.concentration: Some spells last only while you concentrate. You can concentrate on one spell at a time, and taking damage forces a Constitution saving throw to keep it going.
term.ritual: A spell cast as a ritual takes 10 extra minutes but does not spend a spell slot.
term.components: What casting requires: V means speaking magic words, S a free hand for gestures, M a material such as a focus or a component pouch.
term.exhaustion: Levels of deep fatigue. Each level gives −2 to every d20 roll and slows you by 5 feet; at level 6 you die. A Long Rest removes one level.
term.pointBuy: A fair way to assign scores: every ability starts at 8 and you spend 27 points to raise them, up to 15 before bonuses. Higher scores cost more points.
term.feat: A special talent that gives your character an edge beyond class features. Origin feats come from your background; at later levels you can take one instead of an ability boost.
term.multiclass: Taking your next level in a different class instead of your current one. You keep everything you already have; the levels of all your classes add up to your total.
term.weaponSimple: An easy weapon to use without special training: clubs, daggers, spears. Almost every class is proficient with all Simple weapons.
term.weaponMartial: A weapon that demands real combat training: swords, axes, bows. Mostly the warrior classes are proficient with Martial weapons.
term.weaponFinesse: Attack with Strength or Dexterity, whichever suits you: the same ability counts for both the attack roll and the damage.
term.weaponLight: Small enough for two-weapon fighting: after you attack with a Light weapon, you can make one extra attack as a Bonus Action with a different Light weapon, adding no ability modifier to its damage.
term.weaponHeavy: Built for strong hands: you have Disadvantage on attack rolls with it if your Strength is below 13 (melee) or your Dexterity is below 13 (ranged).
term.weaponReach: Adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, including for opportunity attacks.
term.weaponTwoHanded: Requires both hands when you attack with it; the rest of the time one hand is enough to carry it.
term.weaponVersatile: Wield it with one or two hands: with both hands, its damage uses the die shown in parentheses instead.
term.weaponThrown: You can throw it to make a ranged attack, using the same ability modifier as in melee. The two numbers are its normal and maximum throwing distance.
term.weaponAmmunition: Attacking requires ammunition: each shot expends one piece, and after the battle you can recover half of what you used. The two numbers are its normal and maximum range; beyond the first you attack with Disadvantage.
term.weaponLoading: Slow to reload: you can fire only one shot whenever you use an action, Bonus Action, or Reaction to attack with it, no matter how many attacks you could normally make.
term.masteryCleave: Weapon mastery: when you hit a creature in melee, you can swing through with one extra attack against a second creature within 5 feet of it (no ability modifier to that damage). Once per turn.
term.masteryGraze: Weapon mastery: even when your attack misses, the target still takes damage equal to the ability modifier you attacked with.
term.masteryNick: Weapon mastery: you can make the Light property's extra attack as part of the Attack action itself, instead of spending your Bonus Action. Once per turn.
term.masteryPush: Weapon mastery: when you hit, you can push the target up to 10 feet straight away from you, if it is Large or smaller.
term.masterySap: Weapon mastery: when you hit, the target has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
term.masterySlow: Weapon mastery: when you hit and deal damage, the target's Speed drops by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
term.masteryTopple: Weapon mastery: when you hit, you can force the target to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + your attack modifier + Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.
term.masteryVex: Weapon mastery: when you hit and deal damage, you gain Advantage on your next attack roll against that target before the end of your next turn.
term.heroicInspiration: A boon the DM grants for heroic moments. While you hold it you can expend it to reroll any die you just rolled, and you must use the new roll. You can hold only one. Tap the token when you receive it; tap again when you spend it.
term.bardicInspirationDie: A die an ally Bard gives you (d6–d12); you can hold only one at a time. Within the next hour, when you fail a D20 Test, you can roll it and add the result, possibly turning the failure into a success; the die is expended when rolled. Tap the size you receive; tap again when you roll it.
